description: Schema for interpretation exposure items. Each item packages a governed binding (BIND-NNN) into a render-ready form for downstream surfaces. No interpretation is created at exposure time — render_payload is copied verbatim from the binding's attached_interpretation.

exposure_id.description: Stable exposure identifier. Corresponds 1:1 to the BIND-NNN binding it packages. Never reused.

binding_id.description: The BIND-NNN identifier from interpretation_binding.json that this exposure item packages.

source_type.description: Propagated from the binding source_type. Describes the structural layer of the construct.

source_ref.description: Propagated verbatim from the binding source_ref. Identifies the structural construct.

interpretation_ref.description: Propagated from the binding interpretation_ref. Points to the INT-XXX entry providing governed meaning.

render_payload.description: Interpretation content ready for surface rendering. Copied verbatim from the binding's attached_interpretation. Must be identical to the binding source. Consumers must not modify this content at render time.

behavioral_meaning.description: Verbatim copy of behavioral_meaning from the binding's attached_interpretation. For workspace_why and future_ui surfaces.

system_expression.description: Verbatim copy of system_expression from the binding's attached_interpretation. For future_ui surfaces.

business_expression.description: Verbatim copy of business_expression from the binding's attached_interpretation. For lens_report and future_ui surfaces.

render_targets.description: Declares which downstream surfaces may render this item and which render_payload fields each surface is permitted to display. Presence here does not activate any surface — it defines the permission boundary.

permitted_fields.description: Fields from render_payload and source_ref/interpretation_ref that workspace_why may display.
Fields workspace_evidence may display. Restricted to trace metadata — not render_payload fields.
Fields workspace_trace may display. Restricted to identifier and source_ref fields.
Fields lens_report may display. Restricted to business_expression for non-technical narrative.
Fields future_ui may display. Permitted to display all three render_payload fields plus trace metadata.

trace.description: Traceability record linking this exposure item back through the binding to the registry source.

registry_entry_id.description: The INT-XXX registry entry providing the governed meaning for this item.

binding_id.description: The BIND-NNN binding this exposure item is derived from.

registry_path.description: Path to the interpretation registry file.

binding_path.description: Path to the interpretation binding file.

evidence_status.description: Fixed status confirming interpretation originates from registry only. Propagated from binding trace.

constraints.description: Invariant constraint flags. All must be false — none of these operations were performed in producing this exposure item.

prohibited_transformations: Combining multiple render_payload fields into a synthesized claim
Adding severity, risk, priority, or urgency labels
Adding recommendation or action directive language
Adding predictive statements about runtime behavior
Adding client-specific wording or domain names not present in source_ref
Reordering or filtering exposure items to imply relative importance

extension_rules.new_item: New exposure items must match a new BIND-NNN entry. EXP-NNN and BIND-NNN must correspond 1:1.
extension_rules.update_item: Exposure items may be updated only if the underlying binding is updated. render_payload must be re-copied verbatim from the updated binding.
extension_rules.deletion: Exposure items must not be deleted. Deprecated items must be marked with a deprecation note.
